notes: Three .pragma() calls at init; Turso lacks .pragma() API — busy_timeout, journal_mode, foreign_keys must be set via connection string or PRAGMA SQL statements.
Synchronous transaction wrapper via better-sqlite3 .transaction() — Turso async driver requires wrapping in async transaction API; DELETE with dynamic placeholders in subquery.
Synchronous transaction with subquery-based dangling-edge prune. Turso async transaction API required; subquery DELETE is standard SQL.
Transaction-wrapped cascade delete; same sync .transaction() coupling as replaceNodes/replaceEdges.
CTE with ROW_NUMBER() window function in SELECT. Turso supports CTEs and window functions but this is read-only — lower migration risk.
WAL file triplet hardcoded; Turso uses different file layout — WAL/SHM files may not exist or behave differently with remote replicas.
.pragma('integrity_check') returns typed rows — Turso lacks .pragma() API; must use PRAGMA SQL statement and parse differently.
WAL checkpoint pragma via .pragma() — Turso has no WAL checkpoint concept for remote replicas; this recovery step must be replaced or skipped.
Recovery assumes local file-level manipulation of SQLite triplet. Turso remote replicas cannot be quarantined/moved at file level.
File-system rename of WAL triplet; Turso remote DB has no local WAL/SHM to move.
Atomic per-file persistence in sync .transaction() — the core write-path unit for scans. Turso async transaction required; 4-statement atomic boundary is critical for crash safety.
Pre-apply snapshot copies DB triplet (including WAL/SHM). Turso remote replicas cannot be snapshot at file level.
Apply orchestrator dispatches to file-level recovery procedures; both assume local SQLite triplet manipulation incompatible with Turso.
Full apply lifecycle depends on file-level snapshot/rollback. Turso migration needs alternative rollback strategy (e.g., logical undo or point-in-time recovery).
Direct better-sqlite3 driver import; Turso uses @libsql/client — every call site in this module couples to the synchronous better-sqlite3 API surface
Synchronous constructor with no options; Turso/libSQL client is async and uses createClient() with URL+auth — entire initDb call chain must become async
Turso/libSQL has no .pragma() method; busy_timeout must be set via connection config or PRAGMA SQL statement if supported
PRAGMA table_info as SQL text; Turso may support some PRAGMAs as SQL but availability is undocumented — needs adapter shim
better-sqlite3 .exec() runs multiple semicolon-separated statements synchronously; Turso .execute() is async and may require statement splitting
better-sqlite3 RunResult.lastInsertRowid; Turso returns rows array — lastInsertRowid must be extracted from RETURNING or separate query
better-sqlite3 synchronous .transaction() returns a callable; Turso has no equivalent — must use async batch() or manual BEGIN/COMMIT
Read-only constructor options do not exist in Turso/libSQL; readonly mode and fileMustExist must be handled at server/connection level
integrity_check pragma is local SQLite; Turso has no embedded integrity check — recovery procedure CG-RP-001 must be redesigned
WAL triplet file model (.sqlite + .sqlite-wal + .sqlite-shm); Turso uses remote replicas — all triplet copy/move/restore logic becomes obsolete
